definition: Market participants systematically select the wrong historical analogue during commodity-inflation episodes due to narrative availability bias. The 1970s was invoked in >70% of 2022 commentary despite the 2004-08 episode being structurally closer on 5 of 8 comparison dimensions. This creates exploitable positioning errors.
evidence: 2004-08 was structurally closer to 2021-22 on 5 of 8 dimensions
Incorrect analogue selection produces systematic positioning errors with concrete portfolio implications
open_questions: Can a scoring system for structural similarity across episodes improve analogue selection?
Does the bias persist once market participants become aware of it?
